title: GNSS Positioning and Navigation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: GNSS Positioning and Navigation: GNSS positioning: estimate 3D position and receiver clock bias from pseudorange and carrier phase measurements. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.pseudorange.geometry…); recovery is posed as a linear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~10; ionospheric_model_error_m, tropospheric_model_error_m set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: rho_i = |X - X_i| + c*dt_rcvr - c*dt_sat_i + I_i + T_i + epsilon_i
E.world_state_x: receiver position (X,Y,Z) and clock bias dt_rcvr
E.observation_y: pseudoranges rho_i and carrier phases phi_i from N_sat >= 4 satellites

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D_position_velocity is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 10); ionospheric_model_error_m dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: horizontal position < 2.5 m (C/A code), < 10 mm (RTK). Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~10. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |ionospheric_model_error_m|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_sat * N_epoch) per position fix per iteration
